response.justification: The candidate is a seasoned generalist engineer with roughly 15 years of full-stack experience and current hands-on work building MCP code generators and agentic-workflow tooling, which matches a key technology in this role. However, the position requires 8+ years building production-grade AI solutions, and his AI exposure spans only about a year at re:cinq. There is no evidence of vector databases, RAG architectures, LLM evaluation pipelines, or Python, and his customer-facing technical leadership is limited to workshops rather than enterprise delivery, making him a weak fit for a staff-level forward-deployed GenAI role.
strengths: 15+ years of broad production engineering experience with strong TypeScript/Node.js depth and a testing-first craftsmanship culture
Current hands-on experience with MCP (Model Context Protocol) code generators and frameworks supporting agentic workflows
Proven mentorship and knowledge-sharing record through workshops, multi-day courses, and community talks
concerns: Only ~1 year of AI/GenAI work versus the required 8+ years shipping production AI solutions
No demonstrated experience with vector databases, RAG architectures, LLM evaluation/observability, or Python
Little evidence of enterprise customer-facing discovery and delivery leadership expected at staff forward-deployed level, plus an unexplained 2020 employment gap

key_factors.factor: Missing RAG, vector database, and Python skills
